title: <div>&quotI'm choking,&quot she choked, her anvil fists beating into his stone h
content: "I'm choking," she choked, her anvil fists beating into his stone hunched back. He was staring directly into her slowly glazing eyes. Refusing to loosen his grip, he didn't shift his gaze for a moment; if words were able to break free and take flight from behind her tightly wound lips she wasn't choking--besides, that was the whole point: to choke. Pulling the now damp grey bedsheets tighter around her throat, his fingers dug into his palms deeper trying to keep hold, trying to keep the tension ever and constant. She needed to be choked. Needed, not wanted. She would never dare ask for it though, that would negate everything about the choking. It was the loss of control that she needed. It was the taking control that he needed. Her butterfly eyes began to flutter. Fluttering. Fluttered.
context: From document: "I'm choking," she choked, her anvil fists beating into his stone…
